headline: Duke Energy (DUK) Stock Could Be 29% Overvalued After Rate Case Pushback
summary: Duke Energy stock is coming off a solid 48.0% return over the past three years, yet its valuation signals are split, with the Dividend Discount Model (DDM) implying the shares trade at a premium to intrinsic value while market multiples screen the stock as relatively attractive. A 48.0% gain over three years suggests Duke Energy has already rewarded patient shareholders, which raises the bar for any new upside case. Heavy investment in grid upgrades and expanded low carbon generation for...
